Survey Fish Availability: Distribution Maps

Author

Monica Mion & Max Lindmark

Published

March 26, 2026

Purpose

The modelling choice is deliberately simple:

  • restrict to Q3 only,
  • do not include year effects,
  • do not include quarter effects,
  • compare a non-spatial model to a spatial model,
  • map the best-supported Q3 spatial surface.

Load libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(sdmTMB)
library(viridis)
library(sf)
library(sp)
library(terra)

theme_set(theme_light())

home <- here::here()

Read family data

dat <- read_csv(paste0(home, "/Data/survey/survey_family_model_data.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    survey = factor(survey),
    quarter = factor(quarter),
    family = factor(family)
  )

Restrict to quarter 3

# A tibble: 2 × 2
  survey     n
  <fct>  <int>
1 CTS      395
2 IBTS     304

This restriction removes the main survey-quarter confounding problem and keeps the comparison focused on the period where both surveys sample the system.

Family support in quarter 3

# A tibble: 5 × 5
  family           total n_hauls zero_rate surveys  
  <fct>            <dbl>   <int>     <dbl> <chr>    
1 Gadidae        786134.     699   0.0157  CTS, IBTS
2 Clupeidae      248194.     699   0.624   CTS, IBTS
3 Pleuronectidae 230431.     699   0.00143 CTS, IBTS
4 Other          128475.     699   0.00572 CTS, IBTS
5 Scombridae      61851.     699   0.405   CTS, IBTS

Families selected for mapping

We focus on:

  • Gadidae joint IBTS + CTS Q3 map
  • Pleuronectidae joint IBTS + CTS Q3 map
  • Clupeidae IBTS-only Q3 map

Build a prediction grid

Prediction grid check

Helper functions

fit_q3_models <- function(d, use_survey = TRUE) {
  mesh <- make_mesh(d, xy_cols = c("X", "Y"), cutoff = 5)

  if (use_survey) {
    formula <- catch_kg_km2 ~ survey
  } else {
    formula <- catch_kg_km2 ~ 1
  }

  m1 <- sdmTMB(
    formula,
    data = d,
    mesh = mesh,
    family = tweedie(link = "log"),
    spatial = "off",
    spatiotemporal = "off",
    anisotropy = FALSE
  )

  m2 <- sdmTMB(
    formula,
    data = d,
    mesh = mesh,
    family = tweedie(link = "log"),
    spatial = "on",
    spatiotemporal = "off",
    anisotropy = FALSE
  )

  list(mesh = mesh, m1 = m1, m2 = m2)
}

get_aic_table <- function(model_list) {
  AIC(model_list$m1, model_list$m2) |>
    as_tibble(rownames = "model") |>
    arrange(AIC)
}

plot_family_surface <- function(pred, title, subtitle) {
  ggplot(pred, aes(X, Y, fill = est)) +
    geom_raster() +
    coord_equal() +
    scale_fill_viridis_c() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x = "UTM Easting (km)",
      y = "UTM Northing (km)",
      fill = "kg/km2"
    )
}

Gadidae: Q3 distribution map

# A tibble: 2 × 3
  model            df    AIC
  <chr>         <dbl>  <dbl>
1 model_list$m2     6 10716.
2 model_list$m1     4 11076.
✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
✔ Hessian matrix is positive definite
✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
✔ No standard errors look unreasonably large
✔ No sigma parameters are < 0.01
✔ No sigma parameters are > 100
✔ Range parameter doesn't look unreasonably large

Next step

  1. add ICES/land background layers to these maps,
  2. compare these Q3 family maps to harbour seal diet composition,
  3. build a species-level Q3 version for cod, whiting, haddock, plaice, and herring.
